source.title: Tuberculosis: Guidelines for Diagnosis from the ATS, IDSA, and CDC
source.organization: American Thoracic Society, Infectious Diseases Society of America, Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

signals: Guidelines for Diagnosis from the ATS, IDSA, and CDC
IGRA is recommended over a TST
they cannot differentiate between TB and LTBI
active TB needs to be excluded via presence or absence of symptoms

extraction.summary: Guidelines for diagnosis of tuberculosis (TB) and latent tuberculosis infection (LTBI) using interferon-gamma release assays (IGRAs), tuberculin skin tests (TSTs), and mycobacterial cultures. The guidelines focus on selecting appropriate tests based on patient risk factors and interpreting results to differentiate active TB from LTBI.

population.age_criteria: Children under 5 years preferred TST; persons 5 years and older preferred IGRA
population.risk_criteria: High risk of progression (TST ≥5mm), low-moderate risk (TST ≥10mm), unlikely infected (TST >15mm)
population.disease_definition: Active TB with clinical symptoms/radiographic findings vs LTBI (asymptomatic M. tuberculosis infection)
population.exclusions: Active TB must be excluded before LTBI treatment
population.severity_staging: Risk stratification based on progression risk: high, low-moderate, or unlikely to be infected

key_thresholds.context: Induration size for high risk of progression patients
Induration size for low to intermediate risk patients
Induration size for patients unlikely to be infected

recommendations.recommendation: IGRA recommended over TST in persons ≥5 years likely to have M. tuberculosis infection
recommendations.notes: Especially for BCG-vaccinated or unlikely to return for follow-up

recommendations.recommendation: TST recommended over IGRA in healthy children <5 years
recommendations.notes: When LTBI testing is determined necessary

recommendations.recommendation: AFB smear on three specimens for suspected pulmonary TB
recommendations.notes: False results common; negative doesn't exclude TB

recommendations.recommendation: NAAT for first respiratory specimen in suspected pulmonary TB
recommendations.notes: Negative NAAT with positive AFB makes TB improbable

recommendations.recommendation: Mycobacterial culture from suspected extrapulmonary sites
recommendations.notes: Positive reasonable evidence but negative doesn't exclude disease

red_flags: Multidrug-resistant TB contact or risk factors
extraction.follow_up: One culture isolate should be provided to regional laboratory for genotyping when mycobacterial culture is positive
extraction.escalation_path: Rapid molecular drug susceptibility testing for rifampin ± isoniazid in AFB smear-positive patients. Flexible bronchoscopy when sputum induction fails or not feasible. Dual testing strategy may be considered in high-risk patients to increase sensitivity.
